title: cercis siliquastrum subsp. hebecarpa
intro: Cercis siliquastrum subsp. hebecarpa, also known as the Judas tree, is a subspecies of the redbud tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. This deciduous shrub or small tree has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its leaves, bark, and roots are valued for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cercis siliquastrum subsp. hebecarpa are thought to include relief from digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing, thanks to its saponins, flavonoids, and tannins content. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and infusions of its leaves, bark, and roots, which should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
